topic: Dify vs Flowise 2026: Which Open-Source AI Workflow Builder Wins?

key_points: Dify requires at least 4 GB RAM (recommends 8 GB) vs Flowise running on 1 GB RAM
Dify has 8 Docker services; Flowise runs as a single container
Dify is production-ready out of the box; Flowise is better for rapid prototyping
No migration path exists between the two platforms
Dify has built-in worker model with Celery + Redis for async long flows
Dify ships with conversation logs, per-step timing, and OpenTelemetry export
Flowise's worker support improved in 2026 but still feels bolted-on
Flowise is fine for chatbot-style workloads but less proven for batch or pipeline use cases
Dify has version history per flow and publish/draft separation—closest to 'git for prompts'
Flowise added basic multi-user support recently but still leans single-tenant
Dify is recommended for self-hostable platform with Knowledge/RAG workflows and plugin ecosystem
Flowise is better for Apache-2.0 visual canvas with MCP connectivity
Dify positions as complete production-ready LLMOps platform
Flowise positions as lightweight LangChain-based visual canvas
Dify has more polished dashboard UI with role-based access control
Flowise is preferred by developers who like maximum visual programming control
Both offer free self-hosting but have different resource requirements
Dify better for enterprise teams needing compliance features and on-premise deployment
Flowise better for smaller teams or solo developers with limited VPS resources
Dify has more comprehensive RAG pipeline with multi-retrieval strategies and hybrid search

stats.fact: Dify has 106,000+ GitHub stars as of 2026, while Flowise has 41,000+ stars
Dify raised $30 million at a $180 million valuation, backed by LangGenius Inc.
Flowise was acquired by Workday on August 14, 2025
Dify has over 1 million deployed applications on its platform
Dify requires minimum 4 GB RAM (recommended 8 GB); Flowise runs on 1 GB RAM VPS (~$4/month Hetzner)
Flowise cloud pricing starts at $35/month (Starter), $65/month (Pro); Dify cloud starts at $59/month
Dify offers 50+ built-in tools for AI agents including Google Search, DALL-E, Stable Diffusion, and WolframAlpha
Flowise supports over 100 LLMs, embedding models, and vector databases

angles: Workday acquisition of Flowise changes the open-source commitment landscape — can Dify now claim the indie developer crown?
Resource requirements as a decision factor: Dify's enterprise-grade infrastructure vs Flowise's minimal footprint for solo developers and startups
RAG pipeline depth comparison: Dify's production-grade hybrid search vs Flowise's LangChain-inherited flexibility
The 'no migration path' problem — why choosing the right tool from day one matters
Dify as a complete LLMOps platform vs Flowise as a pure visual canvas — different philosophies for different teams
Post-acquisition Flowise: enterprise HR/finance focus vs Dify's general-purpose developer approach
Community health metric: Dify's 2.6x GitHub star lead and what it signals for long-term viability
